              🔴 DEPLOY HALTED — SECURITY GAP (push-review finding, PM-CONFIRMED real; matches
                 memory obs 36306 build-time note):
                matrixProcedure (the tRPC enforcement surface of tenant-rbac-standard §4) is
                DEFINED in server/trpc/middleware/rbac.ts but WIRED INTO ZERO ROUTERS. Grep
                confirms 0 consumers; only enum gates are used (adminProcedure ×68, operator ×6,
                coordinator ×10, reportGenerate ×13, userManagement ×28). Custom-role users are
                anchored at enum role=tenant_admin, so they PASS every enum gate that includes
                tenant_admin -> at the API layer they have FULL tenant_admin capability regardless
                of their matrix. hasPermission(customRoleId=null)->true also makes matrixProcedure
                FAIL-OPEN if ever used as a sole gate (a viewer would pass). Net: the matrix's
                deny-by-default CRUD guarantee is NOT enforced on writes — only nav-hide + route-
                redirect (sidebar.tsx + middleware.ts) restrict custom-role users. A "view-only"
                custom role would still allow that user to create/edit/delete via direct tRPC.
                This contradicts the feature's guarantee AND the standard's required 3rd surface
                AND STATE's own test plan ("FORBIDDEN on ungranted tRPC actions"). No DECISIONS_LOG
                deferral exists. NOT a phased scope -> an incomplete/insecure feature. HELD prod.

              Blast radius of the halt: NONE live. main holds the code but it is DORMANT
              (additive migration; matrixProcedure unused) and NO custom roles exist in any env.
              Staging/prod were NOT touched (data-first gate is manual; prod is manual). Dev only.
